steps.title: Take the square root of both sides
steps.content:
Starting with $(x+5)^2=4$, take the square root of both sides. Remember the $\pm$:
$$x+5=\pm 2$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
From $x+5=2$: $x=-3$.
From $x+5=-2$: $x=-7$.
So the possible values are $-3$ and $-7$.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
Plug each into $(x+5)^2$ and see which gives 4:
  • A) $x=1$: $(1+5)^2=36 e 4$. Eliminate.
  • B) $x=-1$: $(-1+5)^2=16 e 4$. Eliminate.
  • C) $x=-2$: $(-2+5)^2=9 e 4$. Eliminate.
  • D) $x=-3$: $(-3+5)^2=4$ ✓.
D is correct.
